title: The Complicated Truth About Spiders
subtitle: 蜘蛛的复杂真相：恐惧与生态价值并存

paragraphs.en: Spiders make their presence felt in late August and through early autumn. This is the mating season of some of the most common varieties, when male house spiders come out of hidden corners to look for females, and garden spiders reach adult size and spin their most dazzling webs. Yet while the spider is a familiar fixture of nursery poems or songs and Halloween decorations, its relationship with humans is complicated.
paragraphs.zh: 八月下旬至初秋时节，蜘蛛的存在感格外强烈。这是一些最常见品种的交配季节，雄性家蛛从隐蔽角落爬出寻觅雌蛛，花园蜘蛛则长至成年体型并编织出最为炫目的蛛网。然而，尽管蜘蛛是童谣、歌曲和万圣节装饰中的常客，它与人类的关系却颇为复杂。

paragraphs.en: Fear of spiders is common and has serious impacts on the lives of sufferers. Its prevalence appears unrelated to any rational assessment of risk. Spiders in the UK are almost all harmless. Farmland species perform valuable ecosystem services, by preying on insects that are our competitors for crops.
paragraphs.zh: 对蜘蛛的恐惧十分普遍，并对受困者的生活造成严重影响。这种恐惧的普遍程度似乎与任何理性的风险评估都毫无关联。英国的蜘蛛几乎全都无害。农田中的蜘蛛种类通过捕食与人类争夺作物的昆虫，提供了宝贵的生态系统服务。

paragraphs.en: But they are a constant source of human anxieties — with a cultural association with witches and wickedness dating back to the middle ages. Does this perhaps explain, in part, the lack of data about how spiders are faring in our age of ecological crisis? British butterflies are the most studied group of insects in the world, due to the long tradition of collecting and observing them.
paragraphs.zh: 但它们始终是人类焦虑的来源之一——其与女巫和邪恶的文化联系可追溯至中世纪。这是否部分解释了为何我们缺乏关于蜘蛛在生态危机时代生存状况的数据？由于悠久的收集和观察传统，英国的蝴蝶是世界上研究最深入的昆虫类群。

paragraphs.en: But spider conservationists point out that it was only in the 1980s that the classification of house spiders was properly sorted out. And while information about insect populations is gathered by experiments that measure the numbers hitting windscreens or traps, there have been few attempts to count spiders. The huge reductions in the numbers of flying insects can only mean a reduction in spiders' food supply.
paragraphs.zh: 但蜘蛛保护人士指出，直到20世纪80年代，家蛛的分类才得以妥善厘清。尽管关于昆虫数量的信息是通过测量撞击挡风玻璃或落入陷阱的昆虫数量来收集的，但针对蜘蛛数量的统计却寥寥无几。飞行昆虫数量的大幅减少，必然意味着蜘蛛食物供应的萎缩。

paragraphs.en: A recent landmark study identified a 75% fall in insect populations between 1989 and 2016, with pesticide use thought to be to blame along with the destruction of wild areas for development. This means the overall picture for spiders is worrying, as it is for most creatures. But conservationists are most concerned about those varieties that are threatened due to habitat loss and fragmentation, which makes it impossible for them to migrate.
paragraphs.zh: 近期一项里程碑式的研究发现，1989年至2016年间昆虫数量锐减了75%，农药使用以及因开发导致的野生区域破坏被认为是罪魁祸首。这意味着蜘蛛的总体处境与大多数生物一样令人担忧。但保护人士最为关切的，是那些因栖息地丧失和破碎化而受到威胁、无法迁徙的蜘蛛种类。

paragraphs.en: Of around 650 spider species regularly recorded in the UK, the majority thrive in marshes and wasteland. Conservation efforts, often led by determined individuals, have helped some species to recover by reintroducing them to new areas. With rewilding now firmly on the environmental policy agenda, the hope is that in future, spiders will be enabled to migrate by themselves, adapting to climate change by moving along wildlife corridors.
paragraphs.zh: 在英国有规律记录的约650种蜘蛛中，大多数在沼泽和荒地上繁衍生息。通常由意志坚定的个人所引领的保护工作，已通过将部分物种重新引入新区域帮助其恢复种群。随着再野化如今已牢固纳入环境政策议程，希望未来蜘蛛能够自行迁徙，通过沿野生动物走廊移动来适应气候变化。

paragraphs.en: It seems unlikely that spiders will ever attract the same level of human enthusiasm as bees, birds or butterflies, in spite of their unique status as nature's spinners. But as they reveal themselves in all their splendour this autumn, it would be a good thing if more animal lovers recognised the ways in which spiders are simply terrific.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管蜘蛛拥有自然界纺绩者的独特地位，但它们似乎不太可能像蜜蜂、鸟类或蝴蝶那样激起人类同等的热情。不过，当它们在今年秋天尽情展现其绚烂身姿时，若有更多动物爱好者能认识到蜘蛛的绝妙之处，那将是一件好事。
